title: Suction catheter assemblies
abstract: A dual-lumen closed-system suction catheter assembly has a handpiece (2) at its machine end coupled to a suction source (27) and a source (37) of irrigating liquid. The handpiece has a pistol grip shape with a suction control (30) on its upper part (22) that can be operated by the thumb to open a valve (28) in line between the suction lumen (10) of the catheter (1) and the suction source (27). The flow of irrigating liquid to the catheter (1) is controlled by an irrigation valve (38) in the handpiece that is actuated by a trigger (50) when squeezed by the fingers of the same hand.
claims: Claims 1. A suction catheter assembly including a suction catheter (1) having a first lumen (10) for suctioning and the assembly including a second lumen (11) for supplying irrigating liquid to the patient end (4) of the assembly, characterised in that the assembly includes a handpiece (2, 202, 302) towards its machine end having a first port (25, 325) for connection with a source of suction (27) and a second port (35, 305) for connection with a source (37, 237) of irrigating liquid, that the handpiece (2, 202) includes a suction control (30) for enabling or restricting suction applied to the first lumen (10) and that the handpiece (2, 202) is arranged such that the user can both actuate the suction control (30) and control the flow of irrigating liquid to the assembly using the same hand as holds the handpiece. 2. An assembly according to Claim 1, characterised in that both the first and second lumens (10 and 11) extend along the suction catheter (1). 3. An assembly according to Claim 1 or 2, characterised in that the handpiece (2, 202) includes an irrigating liquid control (38) on the handpiece that can be actuated by the same hand as holds the handpiece. 4. An assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, characterised in that the source of suction (27) and the source of irrigating liquid (37) are both external of the handpiece (2). 5. An assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, characterised in that the handpiece (2, 202) has a pistol grip shape with a main body portion (21) that is gripped between the palm and fingers and an upper part (22) on which the user can place his thumb, and that the suction control (30) is actuable by the thumb on the upper part (22) and the irrigating liquid control is actuable by the fingers of the same hand on the main body portion (21). 6. An assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, characterised in that the irrigating liquid control is in the form of a pump actuator arranged to pump liquid (237) from a liquid reservoir (238) to the second port (242). 7. An assembly according to any one of Claims 1 to 5, characterised in that the irrigating liquid control is in the form of a valve (28) arranged to control flow of irrigating liquid from a source (37) of liquid at pressure to the second port (35). 8. An assembly according to any one of Claims 1 to 5, characterised in that the source of irrigating liquid includes a compressible reservoir (303) mounted on the handpiece (302) such that flow of irrigating liquid to the second port (305) can be controlled by compressing the reservoir (303) with the same hand as holds the handpiece. 9. An assembly according to Claim 8, characterised in that the assembly includes a hinged lever (400) extending along the compressible reservoir (303) such that the reservoir can be compressed to dispense irrigating liquid from the reservoir by squeezing the lever. 10. An assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, characterised in that the assembly includes a patient fitting (4) including a wiper seal (44) through which the patient end of the catheter (1) can be extended and retracted, and that the assembly includes a flexible sleeve (3) extending along the outside of the catheter (1) and attached at one end with the patient end fitting (4) and at its opposite end with the handpiece (2) so that the catheter can be manipulated through the sleeve (3).
